reviews.review_text:
Final Fantasy VII (2013 release) is a re-release of a legendary RPG, but this version mainly focuses on bringing the classic game to modern platforms with minimal changes.

The gameplay is still the original Final Fantasy VII experience, which means turn-based combat, Materia customization, and a strong emphasis on strategy. It’s deep and rewarding, but it definitely shows its age in pacing and presentation.

The story is still the main reason to play. It’s iconic, emotional, and full of memorable characters and moments that helped define JRPGs for years to come.

However, this version doesn’t really add much beyond making the game more accessible on newer systems. It’s still the same classic experience, so players should know what they’re getting into.

Overall, Final Fantasy VII (2013) is a great way to experience a legendary RPG, but it’s essentially just the original game on modern platforms. 

Gameplay: 9/10 Great | Overall: 9/10 Great

Didn't expect much going in, finished it with tears in my eyes.
FF7 is the best thing I have ever played and I doubt I'll play anything that will impact me more in a while. For a game from the 90s it really made me realize how far human creativity can go and how ♥♥♥♥♥♥ graphics don't mean anything. 
Cloud, the protagonist, started as an annoying little ♥♥♥♥ and quickly became my favorite video game character ever. Sephiroth, the antagonist, is one of the best written in history and his backstory and goals make him really likeable. It is an understatement to say that both Cloud and Sephiroth are one of the best written Protagonist-Antagonist duos ever.
The cast is a rather weird bunch, but they're extremely likeable and their character arcs are really emotional and awe-inspiring.
The story itself is one of my favorite ever, I love how it goes from Eco-Terrorism and Anti-Imperialism to a Battle of Fates.
Best game of all time, time to watch advent children :P

Hot take, FF7 is a good game. I'm not gonna wax on about its achievements - FF7 is a landmark in video game history and everyone knows it.
It is showing its age though, and the PC port has a few surmountable issues worth mentioning.

I'd recommend getting 7th Heaven, which is a super easy to install and use mod manager specifically for this game. It lets you bypass the required square launcher / login for starters, and retains steam achievement capabilities. There's a few common lists of mods you should install that get parroted, but I'd suggest generally ignoring these, as they either change the game too much or make it look terrible via weird and incongruous art direction.
Get some higher resolution backgrounds (this is a BIG one), a mod to add camera control to the overworld map (it's more impactful than it sounds), and some cheats for toggling game speed/no-encounters (this is a MUST for travelling, and grinding if you so choose). 

The only other advice I think you'd need is:
- There are two missable party members you SHOULD look up beforehand (Vincent, Yuffie), as both are easy to miss, hard to convince, and one of them is narratively important while the other is arguably the strongest in the endgame (though the game is pretty easy overall). 

- The narrative can be a little confusing. Part of this is due to the story being expanded in later entries (overall dubbed "the compilation of FF7", which is hit-and-miss), but it's mostly due to characters being unreliable narrators. The characters don't explicitly lie to you, but they'll stand on a podium and tout their misunderstandings as fact. Part of the story's intrigue is figuring out what's true, and what characters believe - but if you take everything at face value this story can feel borderline incomprehensible.

- There are missable / timed events, items, and story beats. At times, your next goal / intended location will be intentionally vague as a means to have you explore. If you ever feel stuck, don't want to bother with aimless wandering, or you don't want to miss big items and secrets, I'd highly recommend using the jegged (dotcom) guide for FF7 over alternatives (even gamefaqs). 

Otherwise, you're good to go. Play FF7.
Still a good game and a throw back but rough in the eyes on modern displays without mods.

It's kind of sad that this version cannot be enjoyed by new fans of this game or that old fans cannot come back to it if it isn't already in their purchases. This has the hardest achievements by far and it may be the most rewarding experience to complete. Outside of completing this for your retroachievements account. 

I love this game, I'm about to go play like 2 other versions of it (including the new steam version) before I run through the Remake games now that Revelations has been announced.

Final Fantasy VII is a timeless masterpiece that has aged really well for a game made in 1997. Every aspect of the game reflects the passion and care that went in to the development. The story is emotional, atmospheric and mysterious and it's memorable characters remains as one of the best in gaming. While the remakes are excellent and offers fresh perspective on the world and the characters, the original is the definitive way to experience the story.

Now I can't wait for Revelation to be released!

I love this game. I’ve completed it on most systems. I purchased it on Steam to get the achievements unlocked. I just wish Dirge of Cerberus was also on Steam too!

Vincent is my favorite character, and if you want to play as Vincent in FF7 this is the game to buy! The remakes have great graphics, but that is where it ends. In this game all of your progress saved every step of the way, where you do feel you can collect and max out everything.
Fantastic story. Lovable characters. Fun gameplay

I did it! I finally beat FF7! 

It was...fine. I think FF9 and 10 are technically better, but you cannot argue that this is THE granddaddy (err, 7th-in line grandparent). 

I think the last dungeon really soured my opinion on the game (I ran into the save bug without knowing it existed). Still though, the cast of characters, the world, the story - it all really captures you. This game has soul - without a doubt. 8/10

Last time I played this game, was in 1998. Still as great as I remember. If you're a youngin' I understand the graphics can be off putting. But if you're able to look past that, it's one of the best stories ever told.

For some reason this game completely copied the game Final Fantasy 7 Remake, idk why but it's like a much crappier version of Remake so idk why anyone would play this.
Graphics also look weird, must've been made by an indie dev or something...

decided to play this game on ps3 using a crt tv, had the best time with gameplay. played over 100+ hours and reached lvl 99 before fighting sephiroth, finished him off using tifa's final heaven. the fight with sephiroth and cloud was really awesome. 
good game, play it now.

also cloud and tifa are hot

Cannot play offline. Forums say otherwise, but they're wrong. I booted up game once to make sure it worked. Then the next day got on a plane, opened the game, it refused to let me play because I couldn't authenticate with square enix servers.
Okay so I do more research. Apparently you have to make a square enix account and login once then you can play offline any time after that. So I do that, run the game after logging in and making sure square cloud saves are disabled in game settings. All looks good.
I get on the plane for the flight home... "cannot authenticate". Bro this is so garbage. This game is single player and released with no online features. WHY CAN WE NOT PLAY THE GAME WE BOUGHT?
Luckily after 13 years it seems like they finally caved and removed the online requirement... in the form of releasing a whole duplicate version of the game on steam. Not sure how that makes sense at all but okay. Don't play this "2013" version, go get the other version that looks the same on steam I guess (save files aren't even compatible between them. how does that make any sense?).

Final Fantasy VII is an aesthetic dark euphoric experience that can carry you from childhood to an adult and is one of the the most repeat gameplays that I have ever done. Bless you squaresoft from a long time ago. 
Even to this day there are other challenges and activities that can still envoke other increased evaluations in gameplay duration.

I’ve sunk over 350 hours into this game over the years, and honestly? I’m done. I can’t keep pretending that this is some masterpiece of JRPG storytelling.

The biggest issue is the protagonist. Cloud is a hollow shell of a "cool guy" who relies entirely on his backstory as a crutch for his inability to function like a decent human being. People defend him as "deep," but when you compare him to someone like Shinji Ikari—who was actually forced to confront his trauma without being treated like a god-tier hero—Cloud’s "brooding" just looks like lazy writing. Shinji did the chores and fought for survival; Cloud just wallows in self-pity while his support system (Tifa, Aerith, Zack) picks up all the slack.

It’s even worse when you look at other protagonists. Terra (FF6) had a genuine internal struggle but chose to move forward. Zidane (FF9) had every reason to break down, but chose to rely on his friends instead of isolating himself. Cloud just remains a pretentious poser. Even in AC, he’s still acting like a moody teenager. It’s pathetic. Also in Dissidia, Kingdom Hearts, Super Smash Bros and more. Nomura's just pushing the generic isekai protagonist who has the overpowered skillset, the default "badass" cool looks and unearned harem. And you F-ing call this quality writing?

And don't get me started on the fanbase. You can critique any other character, and it’s "fair game," but suggest that Cloud is poorly written, and you get swarmed by fanboys who act like you’ve personally threatened their life.

Square Enix is stuck in a 90s anime loop, churning out either "I'm so depressed/cool" protagonists or generic happy-go-lucky types. It's no wonder the series feels stale. If you want a real, grounded experience, play Parasite Eve. Aya Brea is everything Cloud isn't—she deals with the situation at hand and takes real responsibility. FF7 is just a bloated, overrated relic that people worship more for nostalgia and Tifa’s design than for actual substance. Save your time and play something that respects your intelligence.
This is a masterclass in the jrpg till the end of time
Best version of FF7 taken away by greedy square enix.
The Best Rpg there is, there was, and there ever will be.
"...quite possibly the greatest game ever made."
